kept.text: DAEDALUS: (Greek: Δαίδαλος, “the cunning worker”) was a mythical Athenian inventor, architect, and master craftsman of the Bronze Age. Renowned for his unparalleled skill, he was said to have carved statues so lifelike they could move and see, and to have invented or improved many tools and mechanical devices. He embodies the archetype of the brilliant yet tragic human innovator whose ingenuity both creates wonders and courts disaster.
